units: λ = k² (inverse-length squared); dimensionless mesh coordinates

provenance.verified_against: reference/fixtures/sphere_pec/baseline.json — physical eigenvalues agree to < 1e-5 relative (cross-IR f64 floor per Epic #88). Arpack.jl regular-inverse mode (which=:SM, no sigma) — see cube_cavity.jl::eigensolve_arpack for the Arpack.jl 0.5 friction artifact.

n_tets.description: Number of mesh tets. Strict equality.

physical_eigenvalues.description: Lowest 5 physical eigenvalues after spurious filtering (λ ≈ 1.42 triplet + λ ≈ 3.27 doublet). Acceptance criterion: 1e-5 relative vs NumPy baseline.json.

n_edges.description: Number of global edges. Note: Julia uses first-seen ordering; the global edge count matches NumPy (4512) but individual edge indices differ.

n_interior_edges.description: Number of interior edges (DOFs after PEC elimination). Strict equality.

best_gap.description: Diagnostic ratio λ[n_spurious] / λ[n_spurious-1] (spurious→physical transition). Large value (~1e12) confirms clean spurious/physical separation.

n_nodes.description: Number of mesh nodes. Strict equality (tolerance < 1).

spurious_dim.description: Predicted spurious-mode count = number of interior nodes. Strict equality.

m_int_diag.description: Diagonal of M_int. Per-DOF sub-stage diagnostic.

n_spurious_observed.description: Algebraic spurious-mode count = rank(d⁰_interior) via SVD with 1e-12×σ_max cutoff (Issue #124). Should equal spurious_dim = 368 on this fixture.

eigenvalues_lowest.description: Lowest spurious_dim+8=376 eigenvalues from Arpack.jl which=:SM (regular-inverse mode). Ascending order; first 368 are near-zero spurious modes.

k_int_frobenius.description: Frobenius norm of K_int (interior curl-curl stiffness). Cross-platform f64 floor ≈ 1e-8 relative vs NumPy.

m_int_frobenius.description: Frobenius norm of M_int (interior ε-scaled mass). Cross-platform f64 floor ≈ 1e-8 relative vs NumPy.

k_int_diag.description: Diagonal of K_int. Per-DOF sub-stage diagnostic.

description: Vector-Nédélec sphere-PEC eigenmode pipeline (issue #129, Epic #88 Phase G.4). Julia reference backend for cross-IR agreement against the NumPy canonical baseline.json. Assembly uses the same cofactor Gram matrix formula as reference/numpy/nedelec_local_matrices.py and crates/geode-core/src/nedelec.rs. Eigensolve via Arpack.jl with which=:SM (regular-inverse mode, no sigma) — see cube_cavity.jl for the Arpack.jl 0.5 calling-convention note. Cross-checked against NumPy baseline.json at 1e-5 relative tolerance for physical eigenvalues at generation time.

n_index.description: Refractive index inside sphere_interior; ε_r = n² = 2.25.
